figures.caption: Table 4: Representative instructions generated for two scenarios. NavComposer relies on sequential RGB frames, referencing transient objects, whereas GIST leverages the Semantic Topology for permanent, structural landmarks.
Table 7: Formative Probe Results (𝑁 = 5). Evaluators navigated relying solely on generated verbal instructions.
Figure 2: GIST Semantic Topology: The skeletonizationderived graph providing walking nodes (green) and traversable edges (teal), overlaid with localized products (orange).

Figure 3: Semantic Zone Classification: Free-space pixels are assigned to 18 specific semantic zones via KD-tree voting over the localized product positions (legend displays the top 10 zones by area).

Figure 4: Intent-aware search via the Gemini-powered web interface. Left: A multi-item recipe query (“biryani ingredients”) is decomposed into distinct physical targets. Right: An abstract query (“vegan protein”) demonstrating how the VLM infers alternative semantic categories to enable robust spatial estimation.
